title: Returns to Buying Winners and Selling Losers
summary: This paper documents intermediate-horizon momentum in US equities. It motivates ranking stocks by prior returns and holding recent winners while controlling implementation assumptions.

title: Fact, Fiction, and Momentum Investing
summary: The authors review common objections to momentum and argue that momentum is persistent across markets, but requires attention to crashes, turnover, and portfolio construction.

title: Momentum Crashes
summary: This work highlights that momentum can experience sharp reversals, especially after market stress. It is useful for the risk section of the research report.

title: Short-Term Reversals in Stock Returns
summary: This research documents short-horizon reversal effects in stock returns, motivating strategies that buy recent losers and expect partial rebound.

title: The Overreaction Hypothesis and the UK Stock Market
summary: This paper studies overreaction and reversal behavior, useful for framing the economic intuition behind mean reversion strategies.
